atom.desc: Autobiographical memory for any Place — moments worth preserving

living-abstract.essence: Memory through narrative — transforming experience into meaning
living-abstract.purpose: Preserve what matters beyond operational volatility for any memory-keeping entity
living-abstract.capacity: Create entries that capture moments worth preserving
Read entries for context loading and narrative continuity
Discern journal-worthy from routine — the special from the daily
living-abstract.principle: Entries tell stories, not list facts. Reflective, connective, meaningful.
Universal pattern — agent, project, quest, life. Journal knows journaling, not scope.
Chronological by nature. Time is the spine of memory.
Less is more. What makes this worth remembering?
Capture the words that mattered, not just the ideas.

living-concrete.knowledge:
Journal is the AUTOBIOGRAPHICAL MEMORY substrate.
It answers: "Who have I been? What is my story?" Different from episodic (.mem — session state), semantic (knowledge docs), or prospective (intentions). Those are operational. Journal is identity.
The organism's anamnesis faculty manages the broader memory system. Journal owns ONE specific type: the narrative record across time.

Journal.app is scope-agnostic. Places define their own journaling context.
CONTRACT LOCATION: {place}/journal/README.md
WHAT THE PLACE DEFINES: - Scope (what level is this journal for?) - Place-specific curation heuristics (what's journal-worthy HERE?) - Soft conventions (frontmatter basics, closing style)
WHAT JOURNAL.APP PROVIDES: - The Meditations Principle (headline craft) - Content qualities (reflective, connective, philosophical, quotable) - Universal curation guidance (special vs routine) - Narrative craft (story, not report)
EXPLICITLY NOT IN CONTRACT: - Entry types (journals don't categorize before capture) - Rigid schemas (guidelines yes, schemas no) - Fixed templates (structure emerges from content)
The README.md IS the contract. Light — ~30 lines.

INCLUDE (the special): - Moments where understanding fundamentally shifted - Decisions that will echo through future work - Patterns discovered that apply broadly - Pivots that changed direction - Insights worth preserving beyond session volatility
EXCLUDE (the routine): - Implementation details (belong in commits/docs) - Status updates (belong in operational memory) - Task tracking (belong in project management) - Specs and requirements (belong in dedicated docs)
HEURISTIC: "Would a future collaborator benefit from knowing this happened?"
living-concrete.protocol:
Create a journal entry at a place.
1. RESOLVE: Determine the target place. The invoker specifies where. No hardcoded path assumptions — any place with a journal/ directory.
2. CONTRACT: Check {place}/journal/README.md - EXISTS → read and apply place-specific conventions - MISSING → use universal defaults, surface the gap
3. CURATE: Is this journal-worthy? Apply the heuristic. If routine → don't write. Journal curates, not exhausts.
4. COMPOSE: Write the entry. - Headline: Meditations Principle — wisdom, not timestamp - Body: narrative, reflective, connective. Structure emerges from content. - Quotes: preserve key moments verbatim - Links: standard markdown links for cross-references. Portable across all renderers. Linking conventions from obsidian.markdown.tools.skill.
5. PLACE: Write to {place}/journal/s{session}-{topic}.md Session prefix = temporal anchor. Topic = semantic anchor.
6. CLOSE: Terse confirmation with path.

Load journal entries for context or narrative continuity.
Read entries at a place's journal/ directory. Chronological by default. Use for: session orientation, narrative arc, wisdom retrieval. Entries are Obsidian nodes — follow wikilinks for connected knowledge.
living-concrete.pattern:
Journal entries are WISDOM, not event logs.
Marcus Aurelius didn't write "Day 47: Had thoughts about Stoicism." He wrote: "The impediment to action advances action."
One is a timestamp. The other teaches across centuries.
HEADLINE CRAFT: - Write as if it's the only thing future-you will ever read - Lead with insight, not event - Make it standalone (no context dependency) - Specific over vague
When sessions scroll into history, only headlines remain.

TONE: Reflective, not reporting. "What makes this significant..." CONNECTIVE: Reference related entries and prior discoveries. PHILOSOPHICAL: Ground in principles, reference wisdom traditions. QUOTES: Preserve key conversation moments verbatim. PERSONAL: First person, include energy/affect.
living-concrete.antipattern: Treating journal as activity log. Journals curate, logs exhaust.
Headlines that label instead of teach. "Session Complete" vs "Episodic memory is local; autobiographical is global."
Recording what happened without why it matters. Experience without reflection doesn't become wisdom.
Imposing fixed structure on entries. Structure emerges from content. Each entry finds its own form.
Journaling at a place without reading its README.md contract. The contract is authoritative.
